data.title: Viatris to market Lexicon's sotagliflozin outside of US, Europe
data.description:
• Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) has obtained an exclusive license to market Lexicon Pharmaceuticals’ (NASDAQ:LXRX) drug sotagliflozin outside of the United States and Europe for all indications.
  • Under the agreement, Lexicon will receive an upfront payment of $25M, with the potential for milestone payments plus tiered royalties ranging from low double-digits to upper-teens on annual net sales. Lexicon will retain all commercial rights to the product for the U.S. and Europe.
  • Lexicon will also provide clinical and commercial supply of the drug to Viatris at an agreed upon transfer price, according to a statement.
  • Sotagliflozin received FDA approval in May 2023 to reduce the risk of cardiovascular death, hospitalization for heart failure, and urgent heart failure visit in adults with heart failure or type 2 diabetes mellitus, chronic kidney disease, and other cardiovascular risk factors.

data.title: Healthcare Services discloses cybersecurity incident
data.description:
• Healthcare Services Group (NASDAQ:HCSG) said it has identified a cybersecurity incident, which involved unauthorized activity within some of its systems.
  • According to a filing, the company immediately activated its Cybersecurity Incident Response Process to investigate the activity and has notified law enforcement authorities.

data.title: Phillips 66 to close Los Angeles-area refinery by end of next year
data.description:
Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) said Wednesday it will cease operations at its Los Angeles-area refinery in Q4 2025, affecting ~600 employees and 300 contractors that currently operate the facility.

Phillips 66 (PSX) said it will supply California's fuel market with gasoline from sources inside and outside its refining network, as well as renewable diesel and aviation fuels from its Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex in the San Francisco Bay area.

The Los Angeles refinery produces 85K bbl/day of gasoline and 65K bbl/day of diesel and jet fuel.

"With the long-term sustainability of our Los Angeles Refinery uncertain and affected by market dynamics, we are working with leading land development firms to evaluate the future use of our unique and strategically located properties near the Port of Los Angeles," Chairman and CEO Mark Lashier said.

Phillips 66's (PSX) exit follows California Governor Gavin Newsom's signing of a bill this week requiring oil refiners to maintain a minimum fuel inventory and authorizing state regulators to ensure refiners plan for resupply during maintenance outages to prevent supply shortages.

data.title: Home BancShares Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 misses by $0.03, revenue of $258M misses by $0.9M
data.description:
• Home BancShares press release (NYSE:HOMB): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $258M (+5.1% Y/Y) misses by $0.9M.
  • Net income for the three-month period ended September 30, 2024 was $100.0 million.
  • During the quarter ended September 30, 2024, the Company recorded $18.9 million in credit loss expense.
  • The Company also recorded a $1.0 million provision for credit losses on unfunded commitments due to an increase in the balance of unfunded commitments.
  • Net interest income on a fully taxable equivalent basis was $217.8 million for the three-month period ended September 30, 2024.

data.title: Ambac Financial gets shareholder approval for sale of legacy guaranty business
data.description:
• Insurance holding company Ambac Financial Group (NYSE:AMBC) shareholders voted to approve the proposed purchase of its legacy financial guaranty business to funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management LP, the company said on Wednesday.
  • "The sale represents a meaningful step in a robust and thorough process to maximize the value of our legacy financial guarantee insurance business and enable us to achieve our strategy to transform Ambac into a specialty property and casualty insurance platform," said Ambac President and Chief Executive Officer Claude LeBlanc.
  • Based on the final count from Wednesday special meeting, Ambac (AMBC) received the requisite approval from holders of a majority of the total outstanding shares of its common stock as of the record date, with ~95% of the votes cast to approve the deal.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q4 2024 or Q1 2025 after receiving U.S. and UK regulatory approval. The company agreed to the deal with Oaktree Capital in June.
  • Ambac (AMBC) shares slipped 0.2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Proficient Auto Logistics sees degrade of results in third quarter
data.description:
• Proficient Auto Logistics (NASDAQ:PAL) said it expects that net income for the third quarter of 2024 will be significantly degraded compared to results reported for the quarter ended June 30, 2024
  • As noted during the company’s second quarter earnings call in early August, unit volumes slowed during June with the slowdown continuing into July.
  • Weakness in unit volumes continued throughout the third quarter of 2024, resulting in estimated unit volumes of flat to a decline of 1% compared to combined unit volumes for the third quarter of 2023.
  • Volume underperformance resulted primarily from significantly less dedicated fleet, brokerage and spot buy opportunities, which are typically at premium pricing, the combined result being an estimated $90 - $92M in revenue for the third quarter.
  • Source: Press Release
